original: Soviet space probe Luna 16 lands on earth after 1st unmanned round trip to moon
Periodic Great White Spot observed on Saturn
Volcano Mount Ruapehu (North Island, NZ) erupts
John Young, American astronaut and 9th person to walk on the Moon (Gemini 3 & 10; Apollo 10 & 16; STS-1 & -9), born in San Francisco, California (d. 2018)

East German cyclist Christa Luding-Rothenburger wins silver medal in the women's track sprint at the Seoul Olympics; first athlete to win medals at Summer & Winter Games in same year; won speed skating gold in Calgary
Carl Lewisruns world record 100m (9.92 sec)
American swimmerMatt Biondisets world record 22.14 to win the 50m freestyle gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; his 4th of 5 gold medals for the Games
American heptathleteJackie Joyner-Kerseesets new world record 7,291 points to win the gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; East Germans Sabine John & Anke Behmer take the minor medals
Future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerBabe Ruthbecomes first to hit 50 home runs in a MLB season with a 1st inning blast off Jose Acosta in a 2-1 loss to the Washington Senators
Henri Giffard, a French engineer, makes 1st engine powered dirigible/airship flight with steam power
Underwater volcano explodes under research vessel Kaiyo-maru-5
William Adams, Englishnavigator, first ever Western Samurai who life was the basis for novel "Shōgun", born in Gillingham, Kent, England (d. 1620)
Brad Bird, American director (The Iron Giant, The Incredibles), born in Kalispell, Montana
Steve Whitmire, American voice actor and puppeteer (Kermit the Frog 1990-2016), born in Atlanta, Georgia
James Doolittleguides a Consolidated N-Y-2 Biplane over Mitchell Air Force Base in NY in the first flight to use only instrument guidance to take off, fly a set course and land
USS Enterprise, 1st nuclear power aircraft carrier, launched
Georges Claude, French engineer and inventor (neon light), born in Paris (d. 1960)
Rock bandNirvanarelease their second studio album "Nevermind"
"Jailhouse Rock" single released byElvis Presley(Billboard Song of the Year 1957)
"The Munsters" premieres on TV
Ken Hinton of CFL British Columbia Lions returns a punt 130 yards

Oakland Raiders Jack Tatum sets NFL record with 104-yard fumble return, in a 20-14 win over Green Bay Packers at Lambeau Field, Green Bay Wisconsin; record tied by Aeneas Williams of the Arizona Cardinals in 2000
Fastest English Channel crossing by a relay team set (15h 30m)
Boston Red Sox sluggerBabe Ruthsets MLB season home run record at 28 off Yankee Bob Shawkey in a 2-1 loss at the Polo Grounds in NYC
Oliver McCall TKOsLennox Lewisin 2nd round to win heavyweight boxing title at Wembley Arena in London, England
Ian Serraillier, English children's books author (The Silver Sword), born in London (d. 1994)
John Rutter, English composer, mainly of choral music (Mass Of The Children), arranger, and conductor (The Cambridge Singers), born in London, England
Anthony Newley, British actor and singer-songwriter (Doctor Dolittle; Goldfinger theme; Willy Wonka score), born in London (d. 1999)
Sarah Knauss, oldest American supercentenarian and 3rd oldest person in the world (119 years, 97 days), born in Hollywood, Pennsylvania (d. 1999)
Christopher Columbusembarks on his second expedition to the New World, setting sail with a fleet of 17 ships
German Zeppelin LZ-76 is shot down over England by New Zealand B.E.2e fighter pilot Alfred Brandon, bringing the airship down in a field near Little Wigborough, Essex
ICC Men's Cricket T20 World Cup, Johannesburg: India wins inaugural tournament with a 5-run win over Pakistan; Man of the Series: Pakistan all-rounder Shahid Afridi (91 runs • 12 wkts)
West German swimmer Michael Gross wins 200m butterfly in Olympic record 1:56.94 at the Seoul Games; third career gold medal
Dutch women hockey team wins world championship
2,500 fans seeBabe Ruth's farewell NY Yankee appearance at Yankee Stadium
1stElvisconvention, 2500 fans attend in Cincinnati
Joe Greene, American Pro Football Hall of Fame tackle (Super Bowl 1974, 75, 78, 79; 10 × Pro Bowl; 5 × First-team All-Pro; Pittsburgh Steelers), born in Temple, Texas
Jake Phelps, American skateboarder, journalist (editor Thrasher magazine), born in San Francisco, CA (d. 2019)
Severo Ochoa, Spanish-American physician and biochemist (Nobel 1959), born in Luarca, Asturias, Spain (d. 1993)
"Blind" Lemon Jefferson, American blues singer and guitarist, born in Coutchman, Texas (d. 1929)
Arthur Guinness, Irish brewer and founder of the Guinness brewery, born in Celbridge, County Kildare, Ireland (d. 1803)
Operation Magic Carpet concludes after having transported 45,000 Yemenite Jews to Israel
SultanSuleiman the Magnificentand his Ottoman troops arrive in Vienna, beginning of the siege
African American slave and poet Jupiter Hammon makes his "Address to the Negroes of the State of New York" speech advocating emancipation at meeting of African Society in NY
Frances Harper (née Watkins), African-American abolitionist, suffragist, poet (Bury Me in a Free Land; The Slave Mother), and writer (Iola Leroy), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1911)[1][2]
Frances Ellen Watkins Harper, American poet, abolitionist, suffragist, temperance activist, and novelist (Iola Leroy), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1911)[1]
Nina Bocharova, Ukrainian gymnast (Olympic gold USSR balance beam, team 1952), born in Suprunivka, Ukraine (d. 2020)
Mel Taylor, American rock drummer (The Ventures - "Walk, Don't Run"), born in Brooklyn, New York City (d. 1996)
Rosa Lee Hawkins, American pop singer (The Dixie Cups - "Chapel Of Love"; "Iko Iko"), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 2022)
Ally McCoist, Scottish soccer striker (61 caps; Rangers 418 games, 251 goals), manager (Rangers 2011-14) and broadcaster (BBC, ITV, BT Sport, Amazon), born in Bellshill, Scotland
Dingaan Thobela, South African boxer (WBO lightweight title 1990-92, WBA lightweight title 1993, WBC super-middleweight title 2000), born in Soweto, South Africa (d. 2024)
Danielia Cotton [Brooks], American rock singer-songwriter and guitarist (Small White Town; Rare Child), born in Hopewell, New Jersey
Theodore "Fats" Navarro, American jazz trumpet player, born in Key West, Florida (d. 1950)
Svetlana Beriosova, Lithuanian-British ballerina, born in Kaunas, Lithuania (d. 1998)
Sonny Turner, American singer (The Platters, 1959-70 - "With This Ring", The Metrotones), born in Fairmont, West Virginia (d. 2022)
Lee Aaker, American actor (Rusty-Rin Tin Tin), born in Los Angeles, California
Bernadette Hingley, British Anglican priest, one of the 1st women to be ordained in England, born in Sowerby Bridge, England (d. 1995)
Emilio Castillo, American saxophone player, composer, and bandleader (Tower of Power), born in Detroit, Michigan
Mark Sandman, American singer-songwriter and musician (Morphine; Treat Her Right), born in Newton, Massachusetts (d. 1999)
Pedro Martínezmakes his MLB debut for the Los Angeles Dodgers against the Cincinnati Reds
